title: helictochloa marginata subsp. albinervis
intro: Helictochloa marginata subsp. albinervis, also known as alpine meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to mountainous regions in Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Helictochloa marginata subsp. albinervis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
